prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for nitrobenzene (14 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used doublet multiplicity (charge +1 and −1 respectively). Partial charges were assigned via Mulliken (PySCF). Exact four-center integrals were used (no density fitting). All three SCFs (neutral, cation, anion) converged; integrity status = ok, trustworthy = true. Fukui charge conservation checks passed: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site is atom 4 (C, ipso carbon attached to –NO₂) with f⁺ = 0.49641779. The most nucleophilic site is atom 5 (C, ortho carbon) with f⁻ = 0.10728820. The dual descriptor is most positive at the ipso carbon (+0.51071) and the nitro-group oxygens (+0.377), confirming their electrophilic character, and most negative at N (+−0.474) and the ring hydrogens.

CLI invocation: cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --out nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json
